title: excentrodendron tonkinense
intro: Excentrodendron tonkinense, also known as the Tonkin lancewood, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Excentrodendron tonkinense has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving symptoms of respiratory infections, and has been used to treat skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant often include extracts of its roots and leaves, which are typically used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical creams.
